goals.description: Finish the reading summary by 20:30 to clear the deadline buffer, then draft a brief text to cohort_3 stating I'm too drained for dinner tonight but will catch up on group tasks tomorrow.
goals.rationale: The 'assignment_A' task is already closed (deadline passed), so obsessing over it is inefficient energy waste. My immediate leverage is securing the 'reading_value' completion before its 20:00 cutoff to avoid another extension request. Simultaneously, I need to assert my boundary with cohort_3 regarding the meal; wasting time socializing now only delays my recovery and reduces my capacity to deliver when they inevitably push back. Direct communication saves time and protects my mental bandwidth.

goals.description: Execute a strict 25-minute power nap starting at 21:00, wake up at 21:25, and immediately resume work without checking phone notifications.
goals.rationale: My focus capacity is critically low and procrastination bias is driving me into a spiral of unproductive analysis. A short, guilt-free reset is the only way to regain the executive function needed to finish the reading. Skipping this leads to total burnout and failure; doing it ensures I can hit the high-priority academic targets first thing in the morning.

goals.description: Draft the bare-minimum outline for tomorrow's growth task while lying in bed or during the nap window if possible, just to keep the momentum going.
goals.rationale: This is a low-yield activity that demands cognitive resources I don't currently have. It's better to defer this until after the critical deadline (reading) is secured and the nap is complete. Prioritizing sleep and the hard deadline now creates the space to tackle growth tasks later without compromising my current performance metrics.
